devices.name: ComAp InteliGen / InteliSys NT
devices.summary: Gen-set controller (engine + generator control, protections, synchronising, load/VAr sharing and power management).
devices.source: ComAp InteliGen NT / InteliSys NT Global Guide + InteliConfig configuration tool (verify against uploaded PDF).

settings.name: Nominal Power
settings.description: Rated real power of the gen-set. Reference (100 %) for all power-based protections and load control.
settings.philosophy: Everything expressed in '%' of power (overload, load ramps, base load, peak shaving) is scaled to this value, so it must match the actual set rating.
settings.performance: Set too high and overload protection trips late / load ramps push the engine past its real rating; too low and the set trips or de-rates before reaching genuine capacity.

settings.name: Nominal Current
settings.description: Rated generator current — reference for overcurrent / IDMT protections.
settings.philosophy: Current protections (overcurrent, short circuit, unbalance) are scaled to this value.
settings.performance: Mis-set value mis-scales every current protection — nuisance trips if low, missed faults if high.

settings.name: CT Ratio (prim/sec)
settings.description: Primary-to-secondary ratio of the generator measuring current transformers.
settings.philosophy: The controller scales raw CT secondary current up to real primary amps using this ratio; all current readings depend on it.
settings.performance: Wrong ratio scales every current reading and power calc — kW/kVAr and protections all read wrong by the ratio error.

settings.name: PT Ratio
settings.description: Voltage-transformer ratio for MV/HV measurement. 1.0 when the controller measures generator voltage directly (LV).
settings.philosophy: Scales measured voltage to real busbar voltage on systems with metering VTs (e.g. 11 kV sets).
settings.performance: Wrong ratio mis-scales voltage, frequency-independent V protections, and PF/kVAr.

settings.name: Nominal Voltage (Ph-Ph)
settings.description: Rated line-to-line generator voltage. Reference for under/over voltage protection and the AVR voltage setpoint.
settings.philosophy: 100 % reference for voltage protection windows; aligns with the PQA tool's nominal-voltage compliance check.
settings.performance: Defines where over/under-voltage windows sit; mis-set shifts the whole compliance window.

settings.name: Nominal Frequency
settings.description: Rated system frequency. Reference for under/over frequency and the speed governor setpoint.
settings.philosophy: Sets the governor speed target and the frequency protection centre; ties to ISO 8528 recovery bands.
settings.performance: Mismatch with the engine governor causes a permanent frequency offset and failed recovery checks.

settings.name: Gear Teeth
settings.description: Number of flywheel ring-gear teeth seen by the magnetic pickup (MPU) for RPM measurement.
settings.philosophy: Controller derives RPM from pickup pulse rate ÷ gear teeth; underpins all speed-based logic (crank, idle, overspeed).
settings.performance: Wrong count gives proportionally wrong RPM — overspeed trips or fails to detect crank/run states.

settings.name: Nominal RPM
settings.description: Rated engine speed reference.
settings.philosophy: Reference (100 %) for speed-percentage setpoints such as Starting RPM and overspeed.
settings.performance: Defines the speed band; mis-set skews crank-disconnect and overspeed thresholds.

settings.name: Starting RPM
settings.description: Speed (as % of nominal) above which the engine is considered 'running' and the starter is disengaged.
settings.philosophy: Crank-disconnect logic — stops cranking once the engine fires and accelerates on its own.
settings.performance: Too low → starter stays engaged into a firing engine (gear damage); too high → starter disengages before the engine can sustain itself (stall / repeated cranks).

settings.name: Prestart Time
settings.description: Pre-lubrication / glow-plug / fuel-prime time before cranking.
settings.philosophy: Lets oil pressure build or glow plugs heat before the starter engages, protecting bearings and aiding cold starts.
settings.performance: Too short stresses a dry engine on cold starts; too long delays availability in AMF / black-start scenarios.

settings.name: MaxCrank Time
settings.description: Maximum duration of a single crank attempt.
settings.philosophy: Bounds starter motor energisation to avoid burning it out if the engine will not fire.
settings.performance: Too short may abort before a sluggish cold engine fires; too long overheats the starter.

settings.name: CrankFail Pause
settings.description: Rest time between failed crank attempts.
settings.philosophy: Lets the starter motor and battery recover between attempts.
settings.performance: Too short overheats/over-drains the starter and battery; too long delays a successful start sequence.

settings.name: Crank Attempts
settings.description: Number of crank attempts before declaring a start failure.
settings.philosophy: Balances persistence against protecting the starter/battery and raising a 'Start Fail' alarm.
settings.performance: More attempts improve start reliability on marginal engines but risk flooding/draining; fewer give up quickly.

settings.name: Idle Time
settings.description: Low-speed warm-up period after start before ramping to nominal (diesels with idle control).
settings.philosophy: Warms oil and components at low load to reduce wear before accepting load.
settings.performance: Too short loads a cold engine (wear/smoke); too long delays readiness.

settings.name: Min/Max Stab Time
settings.description: Minimum and maximum time to wait for voltage & frequency to stabilise after reaching nominal speed before closing the breaker / allowing synchronising.
settings.philosophy: Ensures the set is electrically stable before it is connected; Max acts as a timeout that raises an alarm if stability is never reached.
settings.performance: Min too short risks closing onto an unstable set; Max too short nuisance-alarms a slow-stabilising set.

settings.name: Cooling Speed
settings.description: Engine speed used during the unloaded cool-down period.
settings.philosophy: Lets turbo/temperatures settle before shutdown.
settings.performance: Cooling at idle saves fuel but cools turbos slower; nominal cools faster but uses more fuel.

settings.name: Cooling Time
settings.description: Unloaded run time after the breaker opens, before the engine stops.
settings.philosophy: Allows turbocharger and coolant temperatures to fall gradually, extending turbo life.
settings.performance: Too short risks turbo coking / thermal shock; too long wastes fuel and run-hours.

settings.name: Overload (BOC/Wrn)
settings.description: Real-power overload threshold (% of Nominal Power) with a time delay.
settings.philosophy: Protects the engine from sustained loading beyond its rating; usually a warning then a breaker-open/trip level.
settings.performance: Lower = earlier protection but more nuisance trips on transient peaks; higher = risk of engine overload/overheat.

settings.name: Overcurrent (IDMT)
settings.description: Inverse-time generator overcurrent protection scaled to Nominal Current.
settings.philosophy: Time-current curve protects windings while allowing brief inrush / motor-start surges.
settings.performance: Tight settings trip on legitimate inrush; loose settings allow thermal damage during sustained faults.

settings.name: Short Circuit
settings.description: Instantaneous high-current trip.
settings.philosophy: Fast clearing of bolted faults to limit damage.
settings.performance: Too sensitive trips on inrush; too high delays fault clearing.

settings.name: Voltage Unbalance
settings.description: Maximum allowed phase voltage imbalance, with delay.
settings.philosophy: Detects lost-phase / unbalanced load conditions that overheat the alternator.
settings.performance: Lower catches imbalance sooner but nuisance-trips on naturally unbalanced single-phase loads.

settings.name: Gen >V / Gen <V
settings.description: Generator over/under voltage trip levels (% of nominal) with delays.
settings.philosophy: Disconnects the set when AVR/excitation faults push voltage out of a safe window.
settings.performance: Narrow window improves load protection but risks tripping during normal transient dips/rises; wide window can let damaging voltage reach the load.

settings.name: Gen >f / Gen <f
settings.description: Generator over/under frequency trip levels with delays.
settings.philosophy: Protects against governor runaway (overspeed) and prolonged underspeed/overload.
settings.performance: Tight bands fail ISO recovery transients; loose bands allow off-frequency operation of sensitive loads.

settings.name: Reverse Power
settings.description: Reverse (motoring) power trip when the alternator draws power from the bus.
settings.philosophy: Protects the engine when, in parallel, it loses prime mover power and is motored by other sets.
settings.performance: Too sensitive trips on small reverse swings during load steps; too loose allows prolonged motoring (engine damage).

settings.name: Speed Gov Bias / Char
settings.description: Output type/characteristic driving the engine speed governor.
settings.philosophy: Defines how the controller biases engine speed for synchronising and load control.
settings.performance: Wrong type/polarity makes the set chase frequency the wrong way — failure to sync / runaway.

settings.name: Freq Gain
settings.description: Proportional gain of the frequency/speed control loop.
settings.philosophy: Determines how aggressively the controller corrects frequency error toward nominal before/while synchronising.
settings.performance: Too high → frequency hunting/oscillation; too low → slow frequency recovery, longer ISO recovery times.

settings.name: Freq Int
settings.description: Integral term of the frequency loop.
settings.philosophy: Removes steady-state frequency offset so the set settles exactly on nominal.
settings.performance: Too high → slow oscillation / overshoot; too low → lingering frequency error.

settings.name: Angle Gain
settings.description: Phase-angle matching gain during synchronising.
settings.philosophy: Pulls generator phase angle into the synchroniser window before breaker close.
settings.performance: Too high → angle hunting and missed close windows; too low → very slow synchronising.

settings.name: Load Ramp
settings.description: Time to ramp load up after breaker close (and down before open).
settings.philosophy: Soft loading/unloading to avoid frequency dips and mechanical shock on the engine.
settings.performance: Fast ramp causes large frequency/voltage transients (ISO recovery failures); slow ramp delays reaching base/peak load.

settings.name: LS Gain / LS Int
settings.description: Load-sharing proportional / integral gains in parallel operation.
settings.philosophy: Make multiple sets share real power proportionally to their rating on a common bus.
settings.performance: Too high → power hunting between sets; too low → uneven sharing / slow balancing.

settings.name: Voltage Gain / Int
settings.description: Gains of the controller's voltage-bias loop to the AVR (when the controller trims the AVR setpoint).
settings.philosophy: Lets the gen-set controller adjust generator voltage for matching and VAr sharing via the AVR's external bias input.
settings.performance: Too high → voltage hunting; too low → slow voltage matching before paralleling.

settings.name: PF Gain / PF Int
settings.description: Power-factor control loop gains when paralleled to the mains (import/export PF regulation).
settings.philosophy: Holds a target PF / kVAr when running in parallel with the grid.
settings.performance: Too high → reactive-power hunting; too low → slow PF correction, poor reactive sharing.

settings.name: Base PF / VAr
settings.description: Target power factor (or kVAr) setpoint in mains-parallel mode.
settings.philosophy: Defines the reactive operating point when exporting/importing with the grid.
settings.performance: Lower PF target raises reactive current and heating; near-unity minimises reactive loading.

settings.name: Mains >V / <V
settings.description: Mains over/under voltage detection for auto mains-failure start.
settings.philosophy: Decides when utility supply is unhealthy and the gen-set should start and take the load.
settings.performance: Tight bands cause frequent unnecessary gen starts; loose bands delay backup during a real dip.

settings.name: Mains >f / <f
settings.description: Mains over/under frequency detection for AMF.
settings.philosophy: Frequency half of the mains-health decision.
settings.performance: As above — trade nuisance starts against slow backup response.

settings.name: Return Delay
settings.description: Time mains must stay healthy before transferring back and stopping the set.
settings.philosophy: Avoids ping-ponging on an unstable utility supply.
settings.performance: Short → repeated transfers on flickering mains; long → runs the set (and burns fuel) after mains recovers.

devices.name: Leroy-Somer D550 AVR
devices.summary: Digital automatic voltage regulator (excitation control) — voltage / PF / kVAr / manual field regulation with PID, limits and protections; 7A continuous excitation (15A SCC) with USB/CAN configuration via EasyReg Advanced.
devices.source: Leroy-Somer D550 Installation & Maintenance manual (5744 en - 2021.02 / c, ref. S.A.R.L. Moteurs Leroy-Somer). Configuration via EasyReg Advanced software.

settings.name: Voltage Regulation (AVR)
settings.description: Primary mode: closed-loop control of generator output voltage at setpoint by modulating field excitation.
settings.philosophy: Core AVR function — measures stator voltage vs reference and drives the PID to adjust exciter field. Baseline for all other modes.
settings.performance: Single regulation mode active at any time; bumpless mode switching when conditions change (e.g., load → paralleling).

settings.name: Voltage Regulation + Quadrature Droop
settings.description: Voltage mode with reactive droop — reduces voltage setpoint proportional to reactive (lagging) current.
settings.philosophy: For parallel generator operation: makes multiple machines share reactive load stably without fast communication. Each machine naturally backs off voltage as it carries more VAr.
settings.performance: More droop → better VAr sharing but lower voltage with reactive load; zero droop → VAr hunting.

settings.name: Voltage Regulation + Cross-Current Compensation
settings.description: Voltage mode with interconnected CT feedback — senses reactive current on adjacent phase and adjusts setpoint.
settings.philosophy: Alternative to droop for tightly-coupled sets: achieves flat voltage with good VAr sharing via direct current feedback.
settings.performance: Requires correct CT wiring (phase V standard). Mis-wired or over-gained → reactive hunting / instability.

settings.name: Voltage Regulation + Load Compensation
settings.description: Voltage mode with load-current feed-forward — anticipates voltage dip from stator current.
settings.philosophy: Faster voltage response to load changes by predicting the IV voltage drop before it happens.
settings.performance: Improves transient response; rarely used in modern generators (PID already handles it).

settings.name: Voltage Matching (U=U)
settings.description: Holds generator voltage = grid/bus voltage before synchronizing (breaker close).
settings.philosophy: Minimises voltage difference across the closing breaker to avoid reactive inrush current surge and mechanical/electrical shock. Typical tolerance: ±3–5% of nominal.
settings.performance: Without it, close-on-mismatch → high current spike, torque shock, and breaker/mechanism stress. Automatic mode-switch after close to PF/kVAr regulation.

settings.name: Generator Power Factor (PF) Regulation
settings.description: Closed-loop regulation of generator reactive output to hold a target PF when paralleled with mains.
settings.philosophy: In grid-coupled operation, the bus voltage is stiff, so the AVR instead regulates reactive current (and thus PF) to a setpoint. Typical: 0.8–0.95 leading (absorbing reactive from grid).
settings.performance: Lower PF → more reactive current & field heating; must be disabled in standalone mode (would cause voltage drift).

settings.name: Generator kVAr Regulation
settings.description: Closed-loop regulation of generator reactive power output to a kVAr setpoint when paralleled with mains.
settings.philosophy: Alternative to PF mode for grid codes specifying reactive power directly. Useful for voltage support and dynamic stability requirements (LVRT, FRT).
settings.performance: Sets reactive loading directly; affects alternator heating and grid interaction. Sign determines lead/lag.

settings.name: Grid Point Power Factor Regulation
settings.description: Regulates reactive power at the grid connection point (not just at the alternator) using grid voltage measurement feedback.
settings.philosophy: Used in grid-code scenarios (e.g., wind, solar) where reactive support at the point of interconnection is required.
settings.performance: Requires grid voltage measurement wiring and grid-code option module.

settings.name: Field Current (Manual / Excitation) Mode
settings.description: Open-loop direct field current control — user sets desired field amp directly.
settings.philosophy: Fallback mode on sensing loss (safety); commissioning/diagnostics mode. No closed-loop voltage control — voltage follows load & alternator impedance.
settings.performance: Used only for troubleshooting or controlled manual operation. Prolonged use without load monitoring risks over-excitation.

settings.name: Proportional Gain (P)
settings.description: Immediate corrective action proportional to voltage error (measurement - setpoint).
settings.philosophy: Sets loop stiffness: how hard the AVR reacts to a deviation right now. P alone cannot eliminate steady-state error.
settings.performance: Higher P → faster step response but risk of overshoot & oscillation; lower P → sluggish, larger voltage dips on load acceptance.

settings.name: Integral Gain (I)
settings.description: Cumulative corrective action removing steady-state voltage error over time.
settings.philosophy: Guarantees voltage settles exactly on setpoint, not persistently offset. I alone is slow (lag in step response).
settings.performance: Higher I → faster offset removal but slow oscillation/overshoot; lower I → persistent voltage offset after transient.

settings.name: Derivative Gain (D)
settings.description: Damping action proportional to rate of change of voltage error (dV/dt).
settings.philosophy: Anticipates fast transients and damps overshoot, improving stability margin. D amplifies measurement noise.
settings.performance: Higher D → better transient damping but twitchy excitation on noisy signals; too high → control chatter.

settings.name: Overall Gain (G)
settings.description: Master gain multiplier applied to PID output (P + I + D terms).
settings.philosophy: Scales PID response to the alternator's excitation rate sensitivity. One PID profile can suit multiple machine sizes if G is tuned per machine.
settings.performance: Low G → slow response, poor recovery; high G → fast response but risk of instability. Critical for load-step transient performance.

settings.name: Soft Start (Voltage Ramp) Time
settings.description: Duration for generator output voltage to ramp from residual (~5% retained) to nominal setpoint after the machine reaches rated speed.
settings.philosophy: Avoids inrush transients on loads & transformers at startup by limiting dV/dt. Too short → voltage overshoot, transformer inrush current, nuisance trips.
settings.performance: Too short (< 5s) → large inrush on transformer-fed loads; too long (> 30s) → slow availability when set is needed fast (AMF, black-start).

settings.name: Voltage Setpoint / Reference
settings.description: Target voltage the PID drives toward. Often ±5–10% trimmable for load matching and VAr sharing tuning.
settings.philosophy: The 100% reference for all voltage-dependent protections and compliance checks. Offset affects overall system voltage and reactive sharing with parallel machines.
settings.performance: Lower setpoint → smaller voltage excursions on load steps but risking under-voltage trips; higher → larger dips.

settings.name: LAM — Load Acceptance Module (voltage dip)
settings.description: Temporary voltage reduction on sudden large load step to reduce active-power demand and ease engine speed recovery.
settings.philosophy: Key feature for ISO 8528 transient performance: trades brief voltage dip for frequency stability, helping the governor hold speed during block loads.
settings.performance: More aggressive LAM → better frequency recovery but deeper voltage dip (sensitive loads may drop out); disabled → larger frequency dips, possible underspeed trips.

settings.name: Underspeed Volts-per-Hertz (U/f) Protection
settings.description: Reduces voltage setpoint proportionally when generator frequency falls below a knee point, preventing over-fluxing when engine bogs down.
settings.philosophy: Protects alternator core from saturation and excessive field demand during underspeed transients. Characteristic: V = Vnom × (f / f_knee) when f < f_knee.
settings.performance: Higher knee → earlier voltage reduction (better engine recovery, less field stress) but more voltage sag; lower → risk of over-excitation saturation.

settings.name: Over-Excitation Limit (max field current)
settings.description: Ceiling on field current to protect exciter and field windings from thermal damage during sustained high-VAr demand or transients.
settings.philosophy: Typically time-characteristic (e.g., allow 15 A for 10 s, then drop to 7 A sustained). Soft limiter that gradually reduces voltage setpoint as limit is approached.
settings.performance: Too low a limit → voltage cannot be sustained on heavy reactive load (large dips, instability); too high → field winding risk.

settings.name: Under-Excitation Limit (min field current)
settings.description: Minimum excitation level to keep the alternator within its stability/capability curve when absorbing large reactive currents (leading PF in grid parallel).
settings.philosophy: Prevents loss of synchronism / pole slip when the machine is forced to operate at leading power factor (e.g., by grid code reactive requirement).
settings.performance: Too low → loss of synchronism / pole slip catastrophe; too high → limits grid-code reactive support capability.

settings.name: Stator Current Limit
settings.description: Limits voltage output by capping stator current to protect alternator windings from sustained thermal overload.
settings.philosophy: Soft limiter that backs off voltage setpoint if stator current approaches limit. Protects against sustained overload or short-circuit conditions.
settings.performance: Too tight → voltage collapses before reaching full output; too loose → winding thermal stress risk.

settings.name: Field Forcing on Loss of Sensing
settings.description: Automatic switch to a pre-set safe field current value if the voltage-sensing input is lost (measurement wire break, VT failure).
settings.philosophy: Prevents runaway over-excitation (and resulting over-voltage) when the controller cannot see the voltage. Allows fault current to sustain if a downstream short occurs.
settings.performance: Must be enabled for safety. Field-forcing value should be chosen to maintain safe voltage while supporting grid faults.

settings.name: Over-Voltage Protection
settings.description: Detects and responds to sustained over-voltage (excitation/AVR fault, loss of load, voltage transformer fault).
settings.philosophy: Typical actions: alarm → stop regulation (switch to field current mode) or trip. Prevents voltage stress on connected equipment.
settings.performance: Threshold too low → nuisance trips on legitimate transients; too high → over-voltage damage to loads.

settings.name: Under-Voltage Protection
settings.description: Detects sustained under-voltage (loss of excitation, exciter failure, overload voltage collapse).
settings.philosophy: Typical actions: alarm → stop regulation or trigger load disconnect logic. Protects motors from stalling.
settings.performance: Threshold too low → prolonged low voltage damages motors; too high → nuisance trips on normal load-step dips.

settings.name: Under-Frequency Protection
settings.description: Detects prolonged underspeed (engine governor unable to catch up with load demand, fuel system issue, or coupling loss).
settings.philosophy: Triggers alarm and/or load-shed logic. Works with U/f slope to ease engine recovery.
settings.performance: Too high → over-sensitive (nuisance); too low → delayed detection of genuine engine problem.

settings.name: Over-Frequency Protection
settings.description: Detects sustained overspeed (governor hunting, sudden load drop, fuel system malfunction).
settings.philosophy: Triggers alarm and/or frequency-limiting action. ISO 8528 recovery bands define acceptable temporary overshoot.
settings.performance: Too low → trip on transient recovery (common fault); too high → runaway overspeed damage.

settings.name: Open Diode Fault
settings.description: Detects open/failed diode in the rotating exciter rectifier by analyzing field-current ripple waveform.
settings.philosophy: Early warning of exciter degradation before catastrophic excitation loss. Allows planned maintenance vs emergency outage.
settings.performance: Catch early for orderly decommission; late detection → sudden loss of excitation during load.

settings.name: Shorted Diode Fault
settings.description: Detects short-circuited diode in the rotating exciter by ripple analysis.
settings.philosophy: Indicates exciter component degradation. Can lead to partial or full excitation loss.
settings.performance: Early detection allows troubleshooting; late detection → reduced voltage support capability.

settings.name: Reverse Power / Active Reverse Power
settings.description: Detects alternator motoring (power flowing backward into the prime mover), protecting the engine from being driven.
settings.philosophy: Critical in parallel operation: if a set loses prime-mover power, it is motored by other machines, causing engine damage.
settings.performance: Threshold too tight → trips on transient power swings; too loose → prolonged motoring damage.

settings.name: Reactive Reverse Power
settings.description: Detects reverse reactive power flow (capacitive generator feeding reactive into the grid).
settings.philosophy: Less critical than active reverse power; can indicate grid disturbance or islanding risk.
settings.performance: Configuration-dependent; used in grid-code scenarios.

settings.name: Synchro-Check
settings.description: Verifies that grid/bus conditions are synchronized before allowing breaker close (frequency within ±0.5 Hz, voltage within ±10%, angle within ~10°).
settings.philosophy: Prevents closing onto an out-of-sync bus, which would cause severe current surge and mechanical shock.
settings.performance: Typical ranges: Δf < ±1 Hz, ΔV < ±10 %, Δθ < ±20° (configurable).

settings.name: Loss of Sensing Alarm
settings.description: Detects broken voltage-sensing wire or VT failure by monitoring sensor plausibility.
settings.philosophy: Triggers field forcing to a safe value to prevent runaway excitation and over-voltage.
settings.performance: Essential for safety; should trigger alarm and/or field forcing immediately.

settings.name: Voltage Unbalance Protection
settings.description: Detects unbalanced 3-phase voltage (single-phase loss, asymmetric load).
settings.philosophy: Protects alternator from unbalanced current heating. Typical action: alarm → stop regulation.
settings.performance: Threshold too low → nuisance trips (natural single-phase load); too high → heating risk.

settings.name: Current Unbalance Protection
settings.description: Detects unbalanced 3-phase stator current (winding asymmetry, load unbalance).
settings.philosophy: Similar to voltage unbalance; protects windings from asymmetric heating.
settings.performance: Configuration and threshold per machine.

settings.name: Short-Circuit Detection
settings.description: Detects bolted 3-phase short (stator fault) by sudden current rise.
settings.philosophy: Immediate action: typically switches to field current mode to sustain fault current for downstream protective devices to clear.
settings.performance: Allows coordination with switchgear; without it, AVR may reduce field and starve fault current.

settings.name: Power Supply Fault (auxiliary 8–35 VDC)
settings.description: Detects loss or degradation of the DC auxiliary supply powering the AVR.
settings.philosophy: Triggers alarm and/or field-forcing action. Loss of auxiliary power → loss of control.
settings.performance: Essential monitoring; auxiliary must be properly fused (1 A fast-acting typical).

settings.name: IGBT Fault (power stage)
settings.description: Detects power semiconductor (IGBT) over-temperature or over-stress in the field-drive bridge.
settings.philosophy: Triggers thermal shutdown or power limiting to protect the bridge. Indicates cooling or load issue.
settings.performance: Heatsink blockage, high ambient, or sustained heavy transients can trigger this.

settings.name: Temperature Alarms & Trips
settings.description: Monitors D550 PCB temperature (1–5 external Pt100 sensors can be added for field/stator temperature).
settings.philosophy: Prevents thermal damage to the AVR and alternator. External sensor limits protect the machine itself.
settings.performance: Low limits → nuisance alarms in hot climates; high limits → thermal risk.

settings.name: Quadrature Droop Characteristic
settings.description: Voltage droop proportional to reactive current: V_out = V_set - droop% × (I_q / I_rated).
settings.philosophy: Makes paralleled machines share VAr load stably. Each machine naturally backs off voltage as it carries more reactive current, preventing one machine from hogging the load.
settings.performance: Higher droop → more voltage sag but better VAr sharing; zero droop → VAr hunting oscillation.

settings.name: Cross-Current Compensation (CCC)
settings.description: Uses interconnected phase-V current transformers to feed cross-current signal directly into voltage setpoint adjustment.
settings.philosophy: Direct feedback of inter-machine reactive current allows flat voltage + stable VAr sharing. No voltage droop needed.
settings.performance: Correct wiring/gain → smooth parallel operation; wrong polarity → reactive hunting / instability.

settings.name: Voltage Matching (U=U) Tolerance
settings.description: Before breaker close, the AVR adjusts its voltage setpoint to match the grid/bus voltage within tolerance.
settings.philosophy: Minimizes voltage difference across the breaker, reducing inrush current and mechanical shock at close.
settings.performance: Tight tolerance → slow matching, may miss close window; loose tolerance → large inrush on mismatch close.

settings.name: Breaker Status Input (closing confirmation)
settings.description: Discrete input confirming the breaker has actually closed, triggering the mode switch (e.g., from voltage matching to PF regulation).
settings.philosophy: Safety interlock: prevents regulating in parallel mode before the breaker is really closed (risk of over-voltage if mains is lost before close).
settings.performance: Must be wired from the breaker auxiliary contact, not assumed from breaker command.

settings.name: Curve Functions (5-point)
settings.description: Defines one parameter as a 5-point function of another (e.g., kVAr reference vs voltage, field limit vs temperature).
settings.philosophy: Allows sophisticated control logic without external PLC (e.g., voltage support curve matching grid code requirements).
settings.performance: Used for dynamic characteristics & non-linear compensation. Linear segments between points.

settings.name: Logic / Analog Gates
settings.description: Configurable function blocks: AND/OR/XOR, comparators, set-reset, addition/subtraction/multiplication/division, temporization.
settings.philosophy: Allows local decision-making without external PLC (e.g., load shedding logic, frequency-dependent actions).
settings.performance: Chain gates together for complex control sequences. Useful for AMF and grid-code adaptive control.

settings.name: Event Logging & Counters
settings.description: Records occurrence of user-defined events (e.g., over-voltage occurrence count) with optional field-current snapshot.
settings.philosophy: Enables remote monitoring and maintenance trending (e.g., how many times has over-voltage been triggered?).
settings.performance: Useful for diagnostics and predictive maintenance planning.

settings.name: Oscilloscope Function (with USB)
settings.description: Real-time waveform capture and replay of up to 8 signals (voltage, current, frequency, field, etc.) via EasyReg software.
settings.philosophy: Commissioning & troubleshooting tool: visualizes AVR response to load steps, transients, and faults in real-time.
settings.performance: Requires USB connection to EasyReg Advanced; essential for tuning PID and validating behavior.

settings.name: Monitor Window (dashboard)
settings.description: Configurable real-time display dashboard showing voltage, current, PF, frequency, temperature, synchronization status, and fault flags.
settings.philosophy: Remote monitoring window when connected via USB/CAN. Shows current regulation state & performance.
settings.performance: Useful for commissioning, operation, and diagnostics.

settings.name: Second Configuration / 50–60 Hz Switching
settings.description: Stores a second full parameter set that can be switched in via digital input (e.g., for 50 Hz ↔ 60 Hz operation).
settings.philosophy: Useful for dual-frequency sets or for selecting alternate regulation modes on demand.
settings.performance: Allows up to 16 parameters to differ between Config A and Config B.

settings.name: Synchronization Logic Module
settings.description: Evaluates grid voltage frequency, magnitude, angle, and breaker timing to determine synchronization readiness.
settings.philosophy: Part of breaker-closing safety logic; ensures conditions are safe before initiating close command.
settings.performance: Typical thresholds: Δf < ±1 Hz, ΔV < ±10%, Δθ < ±20°.

settings.name: Grid Code Functions (option module)
settings.description: Optional modules for grid-code compliance (wind, solar, DG integration): LVRT (low-voltage ride-through), FRT (fault ride-through), reactive power / voltage support curves.
settings.philosophy: Helps manage transient behavior during grid disturbances per local grid codes.
settings.performance: Requires option license and additional external inputs (grid voltage, frequency, phase).

settings.name: Field Current Rating
settings.description: Maximum sustained excitation current the D550 can supply without exceeding temperature limits.
settings.philosophy: Sets the upper limit on field power delivery and the over-excitation protection threshold.
settings.performance: Short-circuit current (10 s max) is 15 A. Exceeding sustained limit risks heatsink overtemp fault.

settings.name: Voltage Measurement Range
settings.description: Input voltage range for alternator voltage feedback. VT required if alternator voltage exceeds 480 VAC.
settings.philosophy: Measures stator voltage for closed-loop regulation. Over-range can damage measurement circuit.
settings.performance: Configure input type (Y/Δ) and VT ratio (1:1 for direct, up to 500:1 for MV/HV).

settings.name: Frequency Measurement Range
settings.description: Bandwidth for frequency detection from AC measurement inputs. Covers wide range to support variable-speed generators.
settings.philosophy: Allows transient frequency measurement and synchronization logic. Zero-crossing detection.
settings.performance: Narrow range (e.g. ±5 Hz) improves noise immunity; wide range helps during major disturbances.

settings.name: Current Measurement (CT)
settings.description: Generator stator current input via current transformer for power and PF calculations, load sharing, and limits.
settings.philosophy: CT secondary is 1–5 A (range-configurable). Overload capability for short-circuit recording.
settings.performance: CT ratio & burden must be entered in configuration; wrong ratio scales all current-dependent functions.

settings.name: Auxiliary DC Power Supply
settings.description: Powers the D550 PCB logic and field-drive circuits. Must be protected by a 1 A fast-acting fuse.
settings.philosophy: Loss of auxiliary → loss of AVR control. Low voltage → control malfunction.
settings.performance: Must be a regulated supply within ±10% of nominal. Transient dropout > 50 ms triggers fault.

settings.name: AC Power Supply Inputs (PMG/AREP/SHUNT)
settings.description: Powers the exciter field circuit or provides input to rectification. Options: PMG (permanent-magnet exciter output), AREP (main stator auxiliary winding), SHUNT (external AC source).
settings.philosophy: Selection depends on alternator architecture. PMG is most robust (doesn't depend on main AVR).
settings.performance: AC supply must be protected by Class CC fuse (15 A max) or 10 A breaker. Loss of AC input → loss of field power.

settings.name: Operating Temperature Range
settings.description: D550 PCB ambient temperature limits. Exceeding +70 °C triggers thermal shutdown.
settings.philosophy: Heatsink design and cabinet cooling must ensure operating condition within range.
settings.performance: Derate output in high-ambient locations (e.g., tropical climates may need >55 °C derating to protect field windings).

settings.name: EMC / Standards
settings.description: Electromagnetic compatibility: industrial immunity to transients, RF, harmonics; emissions controlled.
settings.philosophy: Shielded cables required outside terminal box (>5 m runs or heavy EMI environment).
settings.performance: Proper grounding and bonding essential for compliance; loosely wired systems may see nuisance trips.

settings.name: Voltage Transformer (VT) Requirement
settings.description: Voltage step-down transformer required for MV/HV generators to safely measure at 0–480 VAC secondary.
settings.philosophy: PT (potential transformer) / VT rated for measurement (typically burden class 0.2 or better).
settings.performance: Burden (VA) must be low to avoid voltage drop on the VT secondary affecting measurement accuracy.

settings.name: Current Transformer (CT) Placement
settings.range: phase U: parallel output, phase V: cross-current & grid, phase W: spare
settings.description: CT placement is fixed per function: U-phase for standard parallel sharing, V-phase for cross-current compensation & grid current, W-phase optional.
settings.philosophy: Wrong placement → VAr sharing failure, incorrect power measurements. Cross-current CT must see inter-machine reactive current.
settings.performance: Ensure all CTs have same ratio and burden. Mismatch causes imbalance in sharing logic.

settings.name: Cable Length & EMC Shielding
settings.range: max 100 m total, shielded if > 5 m outside terminal box
settings.default: < 5 m in terminal box okay unshielded
settings.description: Long/unshielded cables pick up EMI and can cause nuisance trips or measurement errors.
settings.philosophy: Recommend shielded twisted pair for all analog inputs (voltage, current) outside the terminal box. Ground shield at one end only.
settings.performance: Poor shielding → random faults, frequency/voltage measurement noise, false protection trips.

settings.name: Field Winding Resistance
settings.description: Exciter field coil DC resistance. Must be measured during commissioning and confirmed against the exciter nameplate.
settings.philosophy: Low resistance indicates winding short; very high resistance indicates open circuit. Both prevent proper excitation.
settings.performance: Used by EasyReg to estimate field time-constant and for over-current detection thresholds.

settings.name: Earth (Ground) Connection
settings.description: The D550 must be earthed via the dedicated M5 terminal to the machine frame / cabinet earth.
settings.philosophy: All control signals, analog inputs, and the heatsink reference to this earth. Poor earth → nuisance trips, measurement errors.
settings.performance: Must be low impedance (< 1 Ω). Use substantial cable, not a small wire. Test resistance at commissioning.

settings.name: Quick Configuration
settings.description: EasyReg database includes pre-tuned parameters for common alternator models. User selects machine, downloads parameters.
settings.philosophy: Fast path for straightforward setups where alternator is from a known OEM with test data.
settings.performance: Can be refined in Advanced mode later. Good starting point for tuning.

settings.name: Advanced Configuration
settings.description: Manual definition of alternator ratings, excitation curve, measurement configuration, capability limits, protection thresholds, PID gains, I/O mapping.
settings.philosophy: Needed for bespoke or unusual alternators. Requires detailed machine nameplate and test/commissioning data.
settings.performance: Allows complete customization for grid codes, voltage matching, synchronization, and advanced modes.

settings.name: Overcurrent Protection (AC supply)
settings.range: Class CC fast-blow fuse (15 A max) or circuit breaker (10 A max)
settings.description: Protects AC power input from overcurrent (short, fault). Must be rated fast-acting to protect control circuits.
settings.philosophy: Slow fuses (Class T) are insufficient and may not clear in time to prevent damage.
settings.performance: Check fuse rating at commissioning; wrong fuse type → may not protect the AVR on internal faults.

settings.name: DC Auxiliary Protection
settings.description: Protects the 8–35 VDC auxiliary supply from overcurrent (short circuit, battery fault).
settings.philosophy: Loss of auxiliary → loss of control; must be protected to prevent extended short. 1 A is standard for 24 VDC logic circuits.
settings.performance: Use ceramic cartridge fuses (fast-acting). Auto-reset breakers risk repeated shutdown cycles on transient faults.

settings.name: Mounting & Heatsink Cooling
settings.range: vertical heatsink orientation, ≥ 50 mm clearance all sides
settings.description: Heatsink (rear aluminum fin structure) must have free airflow for thermal dissipation. Vertical orientation is best (convection).
settings.philosophy: Blocked heatsink → over-temperature fault, reduced field output.
settings.performance: In high-ambient cabinets, consider duct cooling or forced-air fan.

settings.name: Preventive Maintenance Schedule
settings.range: visual inspection annually, connector tightness check yearly
settings.description: Remove dust (dry air), verify connector tightness (0.6–0.8 Nm terminal torque), ensure heatsink airflow is unobstructed.
settings.philosophy: Running hour meter available via parameter 254.008. Replacement advised after 40,000 hours of operation.
settings.performance: Early maintenance prevents failure-mode surprises during critical operation.

settings.name: Typical Anomalies & Remedies
settings.range: voltage drift, loss of excitation, over-temperature, frozen display, no USB communication
settings.description: Manual lists common failure modes and recommended troubleshooting steps (check VT, CT wiring; verify exciter field continuity; inspect cooling; replace AVR if internal fault confirmed).
settings.philosophy: Structured troubleshooting methodology to isolate external vs internal faults.
settings.performance: Most failures are external (broken wiring, exciter fault, cooling blockage). Only replace AVR if internal diagnostics confirm malfunction.
